steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: Find the outer and inner radii. Outside diameter = 60 in $\Rightarrow$ outer radius $R = 30$ in. Wall thickness = $\frac{5}{8}$ in $\Rightarrow$ inner radius $r = 30 - \frac{5}{8} = \frac{235}{8}$ in.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: Compute $R^2 - r^2$ using the difference-of-squares identity: $(R-r)(R+r) = \frac{5}{8} \times \left(30 + \frac{235}{8}\right) = \frac{5}{8} \times \frac{475}{8} = \frac{2375}{64}$.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: Apply the hollow cylinder volume formula: $V = \pi h(R^2 - r^2) = \pi \times 132 \times \frac{2375}{64} = \frac{313500\pi}{64} \approx 15{,}391$ cubic inches.
